# Test MCP protocol validation and compliance

# Test protocol version negotiation
exec bash -c 'echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"initialize\", \"params\": {\"protocolVersion\": \"2025-03-26\", \"capabilities\": {}}}" | mcpspy --pretty'
stdout '"jsonrpc": "2.0"'
stdout '"protocolVersion": "2025-03-26"'

# Test invalid JSON-RPC (missing version)
exec bash -c 'echo "{\"id\": 1, \"method\": \"initialize\"}" | mcpspy --pretty'
stdout '"method": "initialize"'

# Test method validation patterns
exec bash -c 'printf "%s\n%s\n%s\n%s\n" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 2, \"method\": \"tools/call\", \"params\": {\"name\": \"test\"}}" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 3, \"method\": \"resources/list\"}" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"method\": \"notifications/initialized\"}" | mcpspy --pretty'
stdout 'tools/list'
stdout 'tools/call'
stdout 'resources/list'
stdout 'notifications/initialized'

# Test with mcpdiff for comparison
exec bash -c 'echo "test" > test1.txt && cp test1.txt test2.txt'
exec mcpdiff test1.txt test2.txt
! stderr 'error'

# Cleanup
exec rm -f test1.txt test2.txt
